differences.title: 1. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Runs as a separate server process, accessed over a network.
differences.rightDesc: Integrated directly into the application, no separate server.

differences.title: 2. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Handles many concurrent users and large datasets efficiently.
differences.rightDesc: Best for single-user or low-concurrency applications.

differences.title: 3. CONCURRENCY
differences.leftDesc: Advanced locking mechanisms ensure data integrity with many users.
differences.rightDesc: Limited, can encounter locking issues with simultaneous writes.

differences.title: 4. FEATURES
differences.leftDesc: Supports complex data types, extensions, and advanced SQL.
differences.rightDesc: Implements a subset of SQL, focuses on essentials.

differences.title: 5. ADMINISTRATION
differences.leftDesc: Needs setup, configuration, and maintenance by a database administrator.
differences.rightDesc: No separate administration needed, just the database file.

differences.title: 6. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for multi-user web applications and backend services.
differences.rightDesc: Perfect for mobile apps, small tools, and caching.

differences.title: 7. DATA INTEGRITY
differences.leftDesc: ACID compliant with advanced transactional integrity.
differences.rightDesc: ACID compliant but less robust in high-concurrency scenarios.
